member.name: Luke Akehurst
member.synopsis: Luke Akehurst is the Labour MP for North Durham , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Training: Marketing

writtenQuestions.answerText: The department does not prescribe the use of lead generators for the further education (FE) and skills sector and has not made a specific assessment of the utility of lead generators within the training sector. Training providers are responsible for marke...

writtenQuestions.heading: Children: Protection
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of introducing a child risk disclosure scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government recognises the strength of feeling behind proposals for a Child Risk Disclosure Scheme and has considered the potential merits of such an approach. At its heart, the proposal seeks to ensure that relevant information about risk is shared q...

writtenQuestions.heading: Tax Avoidance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, how many individual disguised remuneration cases are outstanding that need to be resolved by HMRC.

writtenQuestions.heading: Buses: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what steps he is taking through public procurement policy to support UK bus manufacturers to compete for (a) Government contracts, (b) local authority contracts, and (c) combined authority contracts.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government believes that it matters where things are made and who makes them. Public procurement must promote our modern Industrial Strategy, bolster domestic capacity, and protect national security in line with our international trade obligations.Th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Armoured Fighting Vehicles: Production
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment his Department has made of the potential impact of future UK production of the Patria 6x6 armoured vehicle on the economy; and when such production could begin.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department’s assessment of the Finnish led Common Armoured Vehicle System Programme and the suitability of its Patria 6x6 platform in meeting the UK’s Heavy Protected Mobility requirements is still underway. As such, it would be premature to comment o...

writtenQuestions.heading: Standing Advisory Councils on Religious Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what discussions her Department has had with the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government regarding the role of Standing Advisory Councils for Religious Education since the publication of the Minis
writtenQuestions.answerText: The legal duty on local authorities, or any replacement of these entities, to convene a standing advisory council on religious education (SACRE) remain included throughout local government reorganisation. Discussions have been held about the role of SACRE...

writtenQuestions.heading: Standing Advisory Councils on Religious Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment she has made of the impact of local government reorganisation on local authorities' ability to (a) convene and (b) support Standing Advisory Councils for Religious Educations effectively.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The legal duty on local authorities, or any replacement of these entities, to convene a standing advisory council on religious education (SACRE) remain included throughout local government reorganisation. Discussions have been held about the role of SACRE...

writtenQuestions.heading: Religion: Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what guidance his Department provides on the role of Standing Advisory Councils for Religious Education in promoting social and community cohesion.

writtenQuestions.heading: Ministry of Defence: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps his Department is taking to reduce delays and overspend in Ministry of Defence contracts awarded to foreign contractors.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The MOD reduces delays and overspend in contracts, including those with foreign suppliers, by applying strong, proactive contract management throughout the contract lifecycle. This includes clearly defining performance requirements and KPIs in contracts, ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Boeing E-7: Procurement

writtenQuestions.heading: F-35 Aircraft: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether his Department remains committed to the acquisition of 12 additional F-35A aircraft.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer I gave to the hon. Member for Huntingdon (Ben Obese-Jecty) on 10 June 2026 to Question 6574.

writtenQuestions.heading: Diethylstilbestrol: Side Effects
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to improve awareness of DES-related risks (a) among general practitioners and (b) within medical education.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department is working with NHS England to improve awareness of the risks associated with in utero exposure to diethylstilbestrol (DES). National Health Service cervical screening guidance states that women who know or believe they were exposed to DES ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
